mnemonics: THROW - Tell Happening, Raise Object Warning
keyPoints: The 'throw' keyword is used to explicitly throw an exception from a method or block of code.
You can throw either checked or unchecked exceptions, but checked exceptions must be declared in the method signature.
You throw an instance of an exception class, not the class itself (e.g., 'throw new Exception("message")').
commonMistakes: Throwing an exception class instead of an instance (e.g., 'throw Exception;' instead of 'throw new Exception();').
Forgetting to handle or declare checked exceptions that are thrown, which results in a compilation error.
Using 'throw' instead of 'throws' in a method signature to declare exceptions.
